data.body:
[G4 child-consistency audit — parent 72d5 (Migrate the LLM gates onto the workflow engine) × child B2 (6b06: plan-review as an engine workflow)]

Checked all 7 cross-child interaction modes against the WHOLE sibling roster (B1 723c, B3 dc58, B4 101a, B5 f12d, B6 7e48, B-RETIRE 961b, B-DM 5506, B-UX 92fa, 0408). ONE high-confidence finding (dependency gap / conflicting assumption). The other six modes read benign (footnoted).

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
FINDING 1 — Dependency gap + conflicting assumption: B2's plan-review workflow verify/coach `prompt` steps assume the engine-auto-injected variables ({ticket_id, ticket_context, repo_path}) suffice, but the plan-review verifier/coach prompts REQUIRE a `{{plan}}` variable (+ the findings/surviving listing) that nothing in B2's deliverable supplies — so the workflow is NOT live-runnable and degrades to INDETERMINATE. The gap is silently carried by a separately-created sibling (0408), not surfaced on B2's face.
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
- criteria:
  • B2 AC1 ("...aggregate `prompt` verify (one call) → ... `prompt` coach, referencing existing reviewers/*.md prompt ids")
  • B2 AC3 ("...runs end-to-end OFFLINE (FakeAgentRunner) producing a plan_review_verdict-shaped result; a test exercises it")
  • parent AC1 (plan-review runs as an engine workflow: ...aggregate verify -> decide -> coach)
  • parent Success Criterion ("plan-review ... run on the ONE workflow-engine substrate ... validated by offline planned-trace parity")

- location: src/rebar/llm/workflow/gates/plan-review.yaml (the verify + coach prompt steps); src/rebar/llm/workflow/runs.py RunnerAgentStep.run (lines 128–138, the resolve_prompt variable dict); the bespoke prompt-variable construction in src/rebar/llm/plan_review/passes.py (pass2_verify/pass4_coach build resolve_prompt({'plan': plan}) + a findings/surviving listing); src/rebar/config.py:276–283 (gate_engine default).

- evidence:
  • RunnerAgentStep.run (runs.py:131–137) constructs the prompt variables as ONLY {ticket_id, ticket_context, repo_path} (== lint_refs.ENGINE_INJECTED_INPUTS, lint_refs.py:80). No `plan` variable, no findings/surviving listing is provided to resolve_prompt.
  • B2's claimed-met AC1/AC3 (completion-verification PASS comment, 1782447028929322001) wires the verify/coach steps as bare `prompt:` steps referencing plan-review-verifier / plan-review-coach with NO mechanism to supply `{{plan}}` — and AC3 passed only because the offline test uses a CANNED agent (_CannedAgent) that bypasses prompt-template resolution entirely, masking the missing variable.
  • config.py:276–283 documents the live failure verbatim: "the workflow PLAN-REVIEW verify/coach steps supply the prompts' `{{plan}}` + findings/surviving on the LIVE path (today the generic RunnerAgentStep supplies only ticket_id/ticket_context/repo_path, so the workflow plan-review verify degrades to INDETERMINATE live — a B2 offline blind spot the B5 cutover surfaced)." gate_engine default reverted to "bespoke" by PR #42 as a hotfix.
  • Sibling 0408 (open) exists SOLELY to close this gap ("the generic RunnerAgentStep supplies only {ticket_id, ticket_context, repo_path}, not the {{plan}} variable ... the plan-review verifier/coach prompts require ... So review-plan via the workflow engine degrades to INDETERMINATE"). The completion-verifier prompts (B3) do NOT need `{{plan}}`, so B2 inherited B3's "engine auto-injects, so the verify step needs no `with`" assumption (B3 comment 1782423637816890001) without validating it for the plan-review prompts — a conflicting assumption between the two migrations.

- scenarios:
  1. A reader trusts B2 as CLOSED+SIGNED with AC1/AC3 met ("plan-review runs as an engine workflow producing a plan_review_verdict-shaped result"), concludes the plan-review workflow is functionally complete, and does not realize it CANNOT run on the live gate — the central parent goal — until 0408 lands.
  2. The offline parity harness (B4) and B2's own offline test both pass with canned agents, so the missing `{{plan}}` is invisible to the entire offline test/parity safety net; only a LIVE (REBAR_MCP_ALLOW_LLM) run reveals it. The epic's offline-parity-only Success Criterion structurally cannot catch this B2 defect.
  3. If 0408 is deprioritized/dropped, plan-review never actually runs on the workflow substrate live (gate_engine stays "bespoke"), silently defeating the parent's primary Success Criterion despite B2 being green/signed.

- impact: B2's deliverable is incomplete in a way its own ACs (offline-only) could not detect: the plan-review verify/coach prompt steps it authored are not wired to the data their referenced reviewers/*.md prompts require, so the workflow is not live-runnable. The defect is real (objective: runs.py supplies 3 vars; the prompts need `plan` + listings; config default reverted to bespoke), and the remediation is owned by a separate, still-open sibling (0408) rather than acknowledged on B2 — a dependency gap between B2 (which is treated as done) and 0408 (which must finish B2's prompt-variable plumbing before the workflow path can be the default). Note this is a story-internal completeness gap surfaced as a cross-child inconsistency; it is NOT a parent-criterion-uncovered (G3) gap — 0408 + B5 do cover the live cutover in the roster.

- checklist_item: "Check the 7 cross-child modes ... dependency gap ... conflicting assumptions ... each detected mode is a finding."

- suggested_fix: Surface the dependency explicitly so no reader treats B2 as live-complete: (a) annotate B2 that its verify/coach `prompt` steps' `{{plan}}` + findings/surviving plumbing on the LIVE path is completed by 0408 (add a cross-reference B2 → 0408 / 0408 `discovered_from` B2), stating AC1/AC3 held OFFLINE-only and the live wiring is deferred; (b) make 0408's "extend RunnerAgentStep / emit an op carrying the plan text + findings listing (reuse passes.py builders, no duplication)" the explicit completion of B2's prompt-step contract; (c) add a note in B2 (or 0408) that the offline FakeAgentRunner/canned-agent blind spot must be guarded by the live smoke test 0408 introduces, so an offline-green B2 is never mistaken for live-correct.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
Modes read benign (no finding):

(1) Implicit shared state — B2 authors shared surfaces later mutated by B5 (gates/plan-review.yaml dynamic-verify branch + plan_review_grounding op; workflow_ops.py) and 0408 (RunnerAgentStep, the same YAML). These are SEQUENCED (B5 after B4 after B2; 0408 after B5/PR #42), not hidden concurrent mutation; the sharing is acknowledged by the later stories. The {{plan}} plumbing gap is captured as Finding 1, not here.

(2) Conflicting assumptions (other) — B2's DET-block short-circuit (workflow skips LLM on P1/P5/P8) vs bespoke (merges an LLM pass on P1) was a known divergence, but B5 explicitly RECONCILED it (P1/P5 blocks flow det_blocking into decide; precheck short-circuits only for exempt + P8-too-big) and B4 reclassified those scenarios block_divergent→parity. Resolved across the roster; benign.

(3) Dependency gap (other) — B2 depends on B1's ProductionBatchRunner (dep present: 723c blocks 6b06) and epic A's batch/overlay constructs (cross-epic dep on 5766). Both satisfied; the batch step is driven by B1's runner per the seam design note. No gap.

(4) Scope overlap — B2 owns the workflow authoring; B5 layers the agentic-vs-1shot verify branch onto the same YAML; B4 reads B2's trace; B-UX/B-DM build the editor over the same library. Each is a distinct layer over B2's artifact, sequenced, not duplicated ownership. Benign.

(5) Ordering violation — B2 is correctly before B4 (parity needs B2 to exist) and B5 (cutover needs parity). Deps: 723c→6b06→101a→f12d. Consistent.

(6) Consumer impact — B2 is purely additive/offline ("live cutover + signing is B5"); it does not change the live claim gate, so no consumer break at B2's boundary. (The live break is a B5-cutover-time artifact, captured under Finding 1.)

(7) Residual references — B2 references existing reviewers/*.md prompt ids, registry.py, det_floor.py, passes.py; B-RETIRE (961b) explicitly KEEPS passes.py and gates bespoke removal on 0408, so the helpers B2 (and 0408) reuse survive retirement. No dangling reference.

ANTI-FP note: Finding 1 is reported as a single high-confidence finding because the live tree OBJECTIVELY contradicts B2's offline-green completion: runs.py:131–137 supplies exactly 3 variables and no `plan`, the plan-review verifier/coach prompts require `{{plan}}`+listings (per passes.py and 0408's Why), config.py:283 was reverted to "bespoke", and an open sibling (0408) exists solely to finish B2's prompt-step plumbing. The remaining six modes are filtered out under the benign-reading rule.
